Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

We couldn't land this for a few release as-is. If this was put under a version check for, say, 2.18.53.0 then it could land today and activate in the future.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1
> 
> We couldn't land this for a few release as-is. If this was put under a version check for, say, 2.18.53.0 then it could land today and activate in the future.

Actually I was thinking this series would be fine to land everything to master .. it is really our users/infra using l_getidentity_nss that needs the transition and we can revert these as needed for our releases (until we complete transition to l_getidentity and drop l_getidenty_nss)

Patch Set 2:

Maybe 2.16.53 is far enough away, given that nobody on mainline releases is using this at all?  Are you planning to add the deprecation warning into your existing releases to help remove this sooner?

Patch Set 2:

Oh, didn't see your comment before I wrote mine. Yes, in that case removing everything on master is fine.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> Maybe 2.16.53 is far enough away, given that nobody on mainline releases is using this at all?  Are you planning to add the deprecation warning into your existing releases to help remove this sooner?

Yes I will be pushing the files -> lustre and deprecation internally to the l_getidentity_nss. It should be fine to update the new releases to start using 'lustre' so the transition to l_getidenity should go smoothly.

parents.subject: LU-16235 hsm: get a valid cookie for RAoLU request

commit.subject: LU-17139 utils: l_getidentity remove 'files' => 'lustre' alias
commit.message:
LU-17139 utils: l_getidentity remove 'files' => 'lustre' alias

Fully remove the old 'files' alias for 'lustre' and deprecate
'nss_files' alias for 'files' now that it can be used directly.
